LFO routing is fixed per LFO slot — check which destination is selected before adjusting amount
devices.sonic_description: Classic analog-modeled subtractive synthesizer with two oscillators, noise generator, two multimode filters, and two amplifiers. Delivers everything from fat basses to shimmering pads with the warmth and punch of vintage hardware subtractive synths.

Additional dynamics control beyond the built-in compressor — sidechain from kick for pumping
Additional harmonic generation for bass to cut through on small speakers
Bass Mono ensures low frequencies stay centered — essential for club mixes
Prevents bass peaks from clipping the mix bus

starter_recipes.name: Deep Sub

starter_recipes.name: Distorted Bass

devices.sonic_description: Purpose-built bass synthesizer with focused controls optimized for low-end production. Features a streamlined signal path with oscillator, integrated distortion, compression, and filter designed specifically for bass frequencies. Delivers everything from clean sub-bass to aggressive, distorted bass lines with less tweaking than general-purpose synths.

pairs_well_with.reason: Physical modeling sounds bloom in reverberant spaces — adds room context
Resonant peaks from physical modeling often need surgical taming
Percussive transients from mallets can be very dynamic — compression evens levels
Rhythmic delays on tuned percussion create gamelan-like patterns
Adds warmth and harmonic density to thin resonator tones

starter_recipes.name: Tuned Metallic Percussion
starter_recipes.description: Bright, ringing metallic hit for melodic percussion parts

starter_recipes.name: Wooden Marimba

starter_recipes.name: Glass Bells

gotchas: Resonator Type is the most impactful parameter — always choose this first as it defines the fundamental character
High Quality + high Decay can create very long ringing tones that build up and mask other instruments
Material controls harmonic damping, not the literal material — higher values mean brighter, longer-ringing upper partials
Two coupled resonators can create very complex timbres — tune Res 2 to a harmonic interval (5th, octave) for musical results
Mallet Noise adds realistic transient character but can be harsh at high settings — blend carefully
devices.sonic_description: Physical modeling instrument that simulates a mallet or noise exciter striking a resonating body (beam, membrane, plate, pipe, or tube). Produces realistic tuned percussion from marimbas to metallic gongs, and can be pushed into otherworldly resonant textures impossible with sampling.

Drifting pitch adds subtle variation to delay repeats — each echo is slightly different
Light saturation enhances the analog warmth Drift is designed for
Adds rhythmic movement that complements the random drift modulation
Deepens the analog imperfection character with subtle detuning

starter_recipes.name: Deep Sub Bass

starter_recipes.name: Drifting Pad

Voice Mode changes dramatically affect CPU and polyphony — Unison is the most expensive
Shape is continuous, not discrete waveform selection — automate it for morphing textures
devices.sonic_description: Characterful analog-modeled synthesizer with built-in pitch and filter instability that emulates the organic imperfections of vintage hardware. A single oscillator with continuous shape morphing, resonant filter, and streamlined modulation deliver warm, slightly unpredictable tones with minimal parameter tweaking.

devices.name: Drum Rack

key_parameters.name: Pad Note Assignment
key_parameters.description: Each pad is assigned a MIDI note (C1=36 by convention for the kick). Programmatic: use `set_drum_chain_note(track, chain_index, note)` rather than GUI drag. The Live 12.4+ drum-map uses note 36 for the bottom-left pad by default.

key_parameters.name: Pad Volume (per chain)
key_parameters.description: Each pad has its own volume fader on the chain

key_parameters.name: Pad Pan (per chain)
key_parameters.description: Stereo placement per pad — crucial for width on hats/percs

key_parameters.name: Choke Group
key_parameters.description: Pads in the same choke group cut each other off (pair hats so closed cuts open, or group percussion so overlapping hits don't stack).

key_parameters.name: Macro 1-8
key_parameters.description: Drum Rack macros address any parameter across any pad chain. Use for performance gestures (global dust filter, global saturation, pad volumes collected onto one knob).

pairs_well_with.reason: The native glue — shape/boom/transients for the whole kit post-rack. Often the entire mix tool a drum rack needs
Glue the kit into one punch — 2-4 dB gain reduction with a medium attack
High-pass below 30 Hz, notch masking from kick/snare
Add harmonic weight without gain staging pain. Especially on sampled kits that sound sterile
Per-pad sends (build a reverb return) rather than stacking on the master
Tighten room-mic'd one-shots or trim bleed from multi-sampled kits

starter_recipes.name: Empty Kit for Sample Programming
starter_recipes.description: Build from scratch — use add_drum_rack_pad for each slot
steps: Create empty Drum Rack on a MIDI track
For each sample: add_drum_rack_pad(track, note=36+i, sample_path=...)
Verify each pad landed: get_track_info, check chain count
Set pad volumes by role: kick 0.85, snare 0.75, hats 0.4

starter_recipes.name: Sliced-Break Reprogram
starter_recipes.description: Take a break loop, slice it, reroute to a Drum Rack for MIDI programming
steps: Load break to Simpler in Slicing mode
classify_simpler_slices to identify kick/snare/hat roles
Recreate as Drum Rack with each slice as its own pad
Program new pattern — you now have MIDI control of the break

starter_recipes.name: Layered Kick (sub + click + body)
starter_recipes.description: Single kick pad with three parallel instruments for fullness
steps: Drum Rack → pad 36 (kick) → add 3 chains within that pad
Chain 1: sub sine (Operator sine wave)
Chain 2: transient click (short sample)
Chain 3: body (sampled kick tail)
Balance chain volumes — sub first, body second, click last

gotchas: A Drum Rack with no pads produces nothing — loading the container alone is not enough. Always verify at least one chain exists via get_track_info before firing notes
Pad 36 (C1) is the bottom-left in the default view and the conventional kick slot. Live 12's auto-mapping assumes this
add_drum_rack_pad is the ONE reliable tool for adding pads. Using load_browser_item repeatedly to 'add' pads creates nested racks, not siblings
Each pad is itself a chain with its own Volume/Pan/Solo/Mute — per-pad mute is different from muting the pad's chain. Use set_chain_volume for the pad-level fader
Macros must be explicitly mapped — they don't auto-address pad volumes. Use add_rack_macro + map_plugin_parameter on the underlying instrument
Choke groups 0 and 'None' are the same (no choke). Use 1-16 for actual mutual-exclusion grouping
A sliced Simpler inside a Drum Rack pad responds to slice-base pitch (36+N), NOT the pad's own MIDI note. Mis-configured slicing→pad routing is a common silence cause
Per-pad sends can be shown/hidden by the rack's S (Send) button on the chain list. Hidden sends still work, but you can't see them — makes debugging missing reverb very hard
devices.sonic_description: A container instrument that maps up to 128 drum pads — each pad hosts its own Simpler (or Sampler, Operator, or any other instrument plus effect chain) and responds to one MIDI note. Drum Rack is not a synth: its sonic character comes entirely from the samples and devices loaded into its pads. Think of it as infrastructure — the spine that turns a folder of one-shots into a playable kit with per-pad routing, choke groups, and macro control.

Classic phaser on electric piano creates the iconic 70s soul sound
Plate reverb on electric piano is a timeless combination
Evens out the velocity-dependent dynamics for consistent level
Adds the warm amp-like overdrive character of a driven Rhodes
Wah-like filter sweeps on electric piano for funk styles

starter_recipes.name: Classic Rhodes

starter_recipes.name: Bright Wurlitzer

gotchas: Velocity is critical for Electric — it controls not just volume but timbral bark and distortion character
Pickup Symmetry away from center creates asymmetric clipping — this is the source of the 'bark' on hard hits
Damper at 0 cuts notes very short — great for staccato funk but unnatural for ballads
Fork Tine Color and Pickup Position interact — both affect brightness, so adjust together
Best played with a velocity-sensitive keyboard — flat velocity loses the instrument's main expressive dimension
devices.sonic_description: Physical modeling instrument that recreates classic electric piano sounds by simulating a mallet striking a fork (tine or tone bar) with electromagnetic pickup and damper mechanics. Delivers authentic Rhodes, Wurlitzer, and clavinet-style tones with the playability and tonal variation that only physical modeling can provide.

pairs_well_with.reason: Spectral sounds merge beautifully with long reverb — creates immersive washes
Evolving partials create naturally varied delay repetitions
Adds width and subtle detuning that enhances the crystalline quality
Surgical EQ can isolate or boost specific partial ranges for precision
Width control essential — spectral sounds often need mono-bass treatment

starter_recipes.name: Evolving Harmonics
starter_recipes.description: Slowly shifting spectral landscape with organic motion and rich partials

starter_recipes.name: Crystalline Pad

starter_recipes.name: Breathy Texture

gotchas: New in Live 12 — exact parameter names may differ from generic descriptions here. Always use get_device_parameters to discover the actual parameter list
High partial counts are CPU-intensive — reduce for dense arrangements
Spectral synthesis is fundamentally different from subtractive — traditional filter-based sound design intuitions don't always apply
Motion Amount at high values can make the sound pitch-unstable — use carefully on melodic parts
Breath parameter adds noise-band energy that occupies a different frequency space than the partials — useful for realism but can muddy a mix
devices.sonic_description: Spectral synthesizer with direct control over individual harmonics and partials. Allows sculpting of timbres at the partial level with spectral filtering, built-in effects, and organic motion. Produces crystalline, ethereal, and breathy tones that sit between synthesis and acoustic sound in a way no other Live instrument achieves.

devices.name: Granulator III

key_parameters.name: Mode
key_parameters.description: Playback mode. Classic = 2 overlapping grains per channel (G II engine). Loop = rhythmic crossfaded playback synced to clip tempo. Cloud = up to 20 unsynced overlapping grains for drones/textures.

key_parameters.name: Grain Size
key_parameters.description: Duration of each grain in ms. Shorter = more tonal; longer = more recognizable as source.

key_parameters.name: Position
key_parameters.description: Read-head position in the source sample. Automate this for smooth source traversal.

key_parameters.name: Position Random
key_parameters.description: Randomness added to read-head position per grain. Essential for Cloud-mode non-repeating texture.

key_parameters.name: Pitch Random
key_parameters.description: Per-grain pitch detune in semitones. Low values give vintage tape wobble; high values give chord-cloud effects.

key_parameters.name: Grain Density
key_parameters.description: How many grains overlap at once. Higher = denser wall of sound.

key_parameters.name: Spray
key_parameters.description: Time randomization of grain onsets. Prevents periodic artifacts that make granular sound 'machine-like'.

gotchas: Cloud mode with 20 grains + 500ms size is CPU-heavy — freeze the track after print
Real-time Capture samples whatever routes to the track — put on a return to grain a single source cleanly
Position at 1.0 (end of sample) goes silent — keep Position + Grain Size below 1.0 of the sample duration
Grain Size below 20ms starts producing audible tonal artifacts — good for FM-like textures, bad for 'natural' grain
devices.sonic_description: Robert Henke's (Monolake) reimagined granular synth. Three playback modes — Classic (2 overlapping grains per stereo channel, the Granulator II engine), Loop (rhythmic crossfaded sample playback), Cloud (up to 20 unsynced overlapping grains). Full MPE for per-note control of grain size, shape, and position. Real-time audio capture samples any source in one click. The single most-used instrument for evolving texture, drone beds, and vocal-chop clouds in deep minimal / microhouse / ambient.

devices.sonic_description: Ableton's original drum sampler — 8-pad sample player with per-pad pitch, pan, volume, and simple filter/saturator/envelope. Older than Drum Rack but still ships with Live; lighter CPU, simpler interface. Good for quick one-shot drums when you don't need Drum Rack's full chain architecture. The understated alternative when a full Drum Rack is overkill.

Adds width and movement to the already complex dual-engine sound
Additional filter movement layered with MPE expression

starter_recipes.name: MPE Pad
starter_recipes.description: Expressive pad that responds to slide and pressure for live performance

starter_recipes.name: Responsive Lead

Cross-modulation between engines can create very complex, sometimes harsh timbres — start low and increase
The dual-engine architecture means more parameters than simpler synths — use get_device_parameters to discover the full set
Engine Mix is the most impactful single parameter for timbral change — automate for evolving textures
MPE Slide and Press ranges should match your controller's sensitivity — too high can be hard to control
devices.sonic_description: MPE-ready dual-engine synthesizer combining an additive-inspired engine with an FM-inspired engine, blended through a central mixer. Adds analog warmth to counterbalance FM's inherently digital character

starter_recipes.name: FM Bass
starter_recipes.description: Deep, punchy FM bass with controlled harmonics — Algorithm 1 for maximum modulation

starter_recipes.name: Metallic Bell

gotchas: Algorithm choice fundamentally changes the sound — always set this first before adjusting operator levels
Non-integer Coarse ratios (3.5, 7.1, etc.) create inharmonic/metallic timbres — use for bells and percussion
Operator Level as a modulator controls FM depth, not volume — small changes cause dramatic timbral shifts
Each operator has its own ADSR envelope — for percussive FM, use fast decay on modulators
Velocity sensitivity on modulator levels is key for expressive FM patches — higher velocity = more brightness
devices.sonic_description: Four-operator FM synthesizer with 11 routing algorithms that range from pure additive (all parallel) to deep FM chains (all serial). Capable of crystalline bells, metallic percussion, punchy electric pianos, and aggressive digital basses that no subtractive synth can produce.

pairs_well_with.reason: Distortion before phaser for classic psychedelic guitar tone
Reverb after flanger smears the metallic sweeps into a wash
Echo after phaser for spacious, evolving repeats
Layer filter sweep with phaser for complex modulation

starter_recipes.name: Jet Flanger
starter_recipes.description: Classic jet engine flanging effect

starter_recipes.name: Subtle Phase

gotchas: Phaser creates notches in the spectrum; Flanger uses a short delay — they sound fundamentally different
High Feedback in Flanger mode creates metallic, resonant sounds — can be harsh
Doubler mode is designed for subtle widening — don't push Amount or Feedback too high
Check mono compatibility — phaser and flanger can cause significant phase cancellation

devices.sonic_description: Combined phaser and flanger effect with three modes — Phaser (sweeping notch filters), Flanger (short modulated delay), and Doubler (subtle stereo widening). Creates everything from subtle movement to dramatic jet-engine sweeps and metallic textures.

devices.name: Pitch Hack

key_parameters.name: Transpose
key_parameters.description: Pitch shift in semitones + cents on the delayed signal.

key_parameters.name: Delay Time
key_parameters.description: Delay in ms or tempo-synced subdivisions.

key_parameters.name: Feedback
key_parameters.description: Pitched echo feedback. Cascading when transpose is non-zero.

key_parameters.name: Reverse
key_parameters.description: Reverse-plays the delay buffer.

key_parameters.name: Randomize
key_parameters.description: Randomizes pitch and/or reverse per repeat. The Villalobos-texture parameter.

gotchas: Randomize above 0.8 can produce notes wildly out of key — gate with key-detection first if harmony matters.
devices.sonic_description: Versatile pitch-shifting delay for Creative Extensions. Combines transposition, reverse, feedback, and randomization on a single delay line. Lighter-weight companion to PitchLoop89 — less of a spatial architect, more of a per-sound treatment for pitched echoes and random reversal textures. The go-to for Akufen-style micro-stutter on hats and the random-reverse pitched ghosts that Villalobos uses on every track.

devices.name: PitchLoop89

key_parameters.name: Pitch A / Pitch B
key_parameters.description: Coarse pitch shift per voice, in semitones. Two voices mean you can split: one +0.05 cents shimmer, one -0.07 cents detune for dual-detune clouds.

key_parameters.name: Fine A / Fine B
key_parameters.description: Fine detune per voice in cents (±100).

key_parameters.name: Feedback A / Feedback B
key_parameters.description: Per-voice feedback. High values (80-95%) produce infinite-ascending or descending pitch spirals. Shepard-tone territory.

key_parameters.name: Reverse
key_parameters.description: Per-voice reverse playback. Reverses the delay buffer before pitch shift — creates 'reverse swell' tails from any transient.

key_parameters.name: Mode
key_parameters.description: Signal routing: L/R (independent), Sum (both voices sum to mono), Cross (voice A to R out, voice B to L out, stereo ping-pong at different pitches).

key_parameters.name: Sample Rate
key_parameters.description: Buffer sample rate reduction. Lower values add characterful lo-fi grit — emulates the original Publison's digital artifacts.

key_parameters.name: Vibrato LFO
key_parameters.description: Modulates pitch over time. Sine or square waveform; rate in Hz.

key_parameters.name: Position LFO
key_parameters.description: Modulates the read position in the delay buffer. Seven waveforms including random. The source of the 'shuddering glitch' and 'fractured rhythm' textures Henke uses.

gotchas: Feedback above 0.95 can self-oscillate into runaway loop — keep a hand on the mute
Reverse mode introduces latency equal to the buffer length — sync-critical material needs delay compensation
Sample Rate very low (<2000Hz) aliases heavily; character, not hi-fi
devices.sonic_description: Robert Henke's emulation of the 1979 Publison DHM 89 B2 pitch-shifting delay. Two independent pitch-shifting delay voices per stereo channel, each with their own pitch (±24 semitones), fine detune (±1 semitone), feedback, pan, and reverse capability. Sample rate selection, hold function, vibrato LFO (sine/square), position modulation LFO with 7 waveforms. THE tool for Monolake-style detuned delay clouds, Basic Channel spatial signatures, and Arpiar-adjacent shimmered stabs.

devices.name: Prearranger

key_parameters.name: Slot Layout
key_parameters.description: Pre-place empty arrangement clips where you want recorded content to land.

key_parameters.name: Auto-fill Toggle
key_parameters.description: Enable Prearranger — live content records into the pre-placed slots.

devices.sonic_description: Structural pre-planning device from Performance Pack (Iftah). Define clip locations in Arrangement View as empty slots, then perform live and Live auto-fills them with the performance. Transforms live recording into a compositional scaffold. Ideal for sketching long hypnotic structures (deep minimal) or entire sets without mid-performance arrangement decisions.

devices.name: Re-Enveloper

key_parameters.name: Band Split Frequencies
key_parameters.description: Low/Mid and Mid/High crossover points in Hz.

key_parameters.name: C/E Factor (per band)
key_parameters.description: Compression (positive) or Expansion (negative) factor per band.

key_parameters.name: Attack / Release (per band)
key_parameters.description: Envelope-shape time constants for each band independently.

gotchas: Expansion factors above 4.0 can cause clipping — compensate output gain.
Crossover bands overlap by ~6dB — not a surgical splitter.
devices.sonic_description: Multiband envelope processor with three adjustable frequency bands. Unlike compression (which reduces amplitude when over threshold), Re-Enveloper reshapes envelope character itself — attack, sustain, decay — independently per band. The right tool for 'shorten the decay of this sustained sound so it behaves like a stab' or 'expand the transient on the highs only'.

devices.name: Redux

key_parameters.name: Bit Depth
key_parameters.description: Reduces the number of amplitude levels — lower values = more quantization noise

key_parameters.name: Downsample
key_parameters.description: Reduces the effective sample rate — creates aliasing and frequency folding

key_parameters.name: Downsample Mode
key_parameters.description: How downsampling is applied — Soft is smoother, Hard is more aliased

key_parameters.name: Jitter
key_parameters.description: Randomizes the sample rate reduction for noisy, unstable character

key_parameters.name: Dry/Wet
key_parameters.description: Mix between clean and reduced signal

pairs_well_with.reason: Layer Redux with Erosion for full digital degradation
Warm saturation before Redux softens the harsh digital artifacts
Filter after Redux to tame harsh aliasing frequencies
Cut high frequencies after downsampling to control aliasing

starter_recipes.name: Lo-Fi 12-Bit
starter_recipes.description: Subtle quality reduction for lo-fi hip-hop or chillwave aesthetics

starter_recipes.name: Extreme Bitcrush
starter_recipes.description: Heavy bit and sample rate reduction for glitch and experimental

starter_recipes.name: Retro Gaming
starter_recipes.description: 8-bit chiptune character for retro game-style sounds

gotchas: Bit Depth of 1 is extreme — the signal becomes a square wave at the input frequency
Downsampling creates aliasing that folds frequencies back down — can produce unexpected tones
Hard mode produces more aliasing artifacts; Soft mode smooths the downsampling
Very heavy settings can make the audio unrecognizable — use Dry/Wet for parallel blending
Low Bit Depth adds significant noise floor — compensate with a noise gate if needed
Jitter adds randomness to the sample rate — creates a more organic, unstable character
devices.sonic_description: Bit reduction and downsampling effect that reduces audio fidelity by quantizing amplitude (bit depth) and reducing sample rate. Creates classic retro digital sounds, from subtle lo-fi warmth at mild settings to extreme 1-bit crunchy noise at aggressive settings.

devices.name: Resonators

key_parameters.name: Fine (I-V)

key_parameters.name: Brightness
key_parameters.description: High-frequency content of all resonators

key_parameters.name: Color

key_parameters.name: Dry/Wet
key_parameters.description: Mix between dry signal and resonator output

key_parameters.name: Width
key_parameters.description: Stereo spread of the resonators

pairs_well_with.reason: Drum hits excite resonators to create pitched percussion
Spatializes resonator output for immersive drone textures
Filter sweeps over resonator harmonics for movement
Controls resonance dynamics and prevents buildup
Gain staging to manage resonator output levels

starter_recipes.name: Harmonic Series
starter_recipes.description: Tune five resonators to the harmonic series (fundamental, octave, fifth, octave, major third)

starter_recipes.name: Chord Resonance
starter_recipes.description: Tune resonators to a chord — incoming audio excites the chord

starter_recipes.name: Drone Generator
starter_recipes.description: Very long decay resonators fed by any audio — creates sustained drones

starter_recipes.name: Percussive Resonance
starter_recipes.description: Short decay resonators tuned to a scale for melodic percussion

gotchas: Each resonator has its own Note, Fine, Gain, and Decay — set all five independently
Very long Decay values with loud input can cause resonance buildup and volume spikes
Resonators respond to ANY input — even silence with noise floor can excite long-decay resonators
The five resonators are parallel, not serial — they all process the same input simultaneously
Low Note values (bass) with long Decay can cause low-frequency buildup — use with Utility for gain control
Width spreads the five resonators across the stereo field — 0 is mono, 1 is full spread
devices.sonic_description: Five parallel tuned resonators, each individually tunable by note, fine pitch, gain, and decay time. Creates pitched resonances from incoming audio, turning noise, drums, or any sound into tuned harmonic content. Can produce drone-like sustains, harmonic series, and chord-based resonant effects.

devices.name: Reverb

key_parameters.description: Length of the reverb tail in seconds

key_parameters.name: Diffusion

key_parameters.name: Low Cut

key_parameters.name: Chorus Amount
key_parameters.description: Chorus modulation on the reverb tail — adds movement

key_parameters.name: Freeze
key_parameters.description: Infinitely sustains the current reverb — great for drones and transitions

key_parameters.name: Dry/Wet
key_parameters.description: Mix between dry and reverb signal

pairs_well_with.reason: Cut low-end before reverb to prevent muddiness; shape reverb tone after
Delay before reverb for depth, or reverb before delay for washy echoes
Compress the reverb return for controlled, even tails
Width control on the reverb return — mono reverb sits tighter in the mix

starter_recipes.name: Small Room
starter_recipes.description: Tight, natural-sounding room for drums or guitars

starter_recipes.name: Large Hall
starter_recipes.description: Spacious concert hall for orchestral or cinematic sounds

starter_recipes.name: Infinite Drone
starter_recipes.description: Sustained reverb cloud for ambient and drone textures

starter_recipes.name: Vocal Plate
starter_recipes.description: Bright, clear reverb for vocals with pre-delay separation

Chorus Amount adds modulation but also subtle pitch variation — turn off for surgical reverb

devices.sonic_description: Algorithmic reverb that simulates acoustic spaces from small rooms to infinite drones. Features independent early reflection and diffuse tail controls, input filtering, chorus modulation, and a Freeze function that infinitely sustains the current reverb cloud.

New in Live 12 — no equivalent in earlier versions
devices.sonic_description: Multi-stage distortion and saturation processor with parallel routing options introduced in Live 12. Features multiple distortion algorithms that can be chained or run in parallel, offering everything from subtle warming to extreme textural destruction with sophisticated routing and feedback options.

devices.name: Saturator

Precise level control after saturation
Filter after saturation to control harsh harmonics

starter_recipes.name: Warm Analog Saturation
starter_recipes.description: Subtle tape-like warmth for any source

Digital Clip is harsh and unforgiving — use intentionally for industrial or lo-fi sounds

devices.sonic_description: Versatile waveshaping saturation with six curve types ranging from warm analog clipping to aggressive digital distortion and wavefold synthesis. Adds harmonics, warmth, and edge to any source with precise control over the distortion character.

Delay + pitch shift = shimmer effect
Tame harsh harmonics from ring mod or frequency shifting

Frequency Shift mode shifts in Hz, not semitones — even small values create inharmonic content
Ring Mod at low frequencies (1-10 Hz) creates tremolo; at higher frequencies creates new sidebands

devices.sonic_description: Pitch shifter, frequency shifter, and ring modulator in one device. Pitch Shift mode transposes in semitones (harmonic). Frequency Shift mode shifts in Hz (inharmonic, metallic). Ring Mod mode multiplies the input with an oscillator for bell-like, metallic timbres. Renamed from Frequency Shifter in Live 12.

devices.name: Spectral Blur

Narrow frequency ranges at high levels self-oscillate — keep Residual > 0 or compress
devices.sonic_description: Spectral halo/reverb hybrid. Creates reverb-like textures by blurring user-defined frequency ranges into dense clouds of delayed/smeared harmonic content. Not a real reverb — no IR — but the result behaves like one, with more control over which frequency ranges get haloed. Freeze button holds the current spectral content indefinitely. The tool for ethereal pads from sparse sources and alien textures that don't sound like rooms.

Control dynamic surges from the resonance process

devices.sonic_description: Essential mixing and signal management tool providing gain adjustment, stereo width control, channel mode selection, bass mono filtering, and DC offset correction. devices.sonic_description: MIDI device (Max for Live) that applies microtonal tunings to downstream instruments. Imports Scala (.scl) files — the standard format for microtonal tunings, freely available from the Huygens-Fokker archive. Supports pitch count up to 128, arbitrary octave ratios, Lead/Follow mode to sync scales across multiple Microtuner instances, polyphonic playback, MPE compatibility. Real-time blend-two-scales slider for shapeshifting melodies and recasted chords.

devices.sonic_description: M4L polyrhythmic MIDI generator from CV Tools. Originally a modular CV-triggers tool, but operates as a standalone MIDI generator inside Live. Generates rotating polyrhythmic patterns across multiple tracks or drum-rack pads. One of the most under-utilized M4L devices for complex techno / IDM groove programming.

key_parameters.name: Base
key_parameters.description: Root note of the scale

key_parameters.name: Scale Type
key_parameters.description: Musical scale or mode to enforce

pairs_well_with.reason: Place Scale after Arpeggiator to ensure arpeggiated notes stay in key
Scale corrects random pitch offsets to stay musically coherent
Corrects parallel intervals that fall outside the target scale
Ensures pitch-shifted echo repeats stay in key

starter_recipes.name: Lock to Natural Minor
starter_recipes.description: Constrain all input to A natural minor — safe for most melancholic productions

starter_recipes.name: Pentatonic Filter
starter_recipes.description: Reduce to pentatonic for universally consonant generative output

starter_recipes.name: Modal Constraint — Dorian
starter_recipes.description: Force Dorian mode for jazzy, sophisticated minor tonality

starter_recipes.name: Blues Scale Lock
starter_recipes.description: Constrain to blues scale for authentic blues/soul feel

gotchas: Scale has a per-note mapping grid — you can customize individual note routing beyond the preset scales
Place Scale AFTER pitch-generating MIDI effects (Arpeggiator, Random, Chord) so it corrects their output
Chromatic scale type passes all notes through unchanged — useful as a bypass without removing the device
Live 12 clips have their own scale setting — the Scale MIDI effect is independent and can conflict. Check both
Fold mode determines whether notes snap to nearest scale tone or are silenced — Fold On is usually preferred
devices.sonic_description: Forces all incoming MIDI notes to conform to a selected musical scale, correcting out-of-key notes by mapping them to the nearest scale degree. Essential for keeping generative, randomized, or improvised MIDI output harmonically coherent.

devices.name: SQ Sequencer

key_parameters.name: Per-Lane Step Count
key_parameters.description: Each of up to ~8 lanes has its own step length (1-16). Non-coincident values = polymeter.

key_parameters.name: Per-Lane Direction
key_parameters.description: Forward / Backward / Ping-Pong / Random per lane.

key_parameters.name: Condition
key_parameters.description: Rule-based gating — fire on every Nth pass, after N fails, etc.

key_parameters.name: Output Pitch / Velocity / Gate Length (per lane)
key_parameters.description: Independent control per lane.

devices.sonic_description: 16-step sequencer with per-lane independent step length and direction — the polymetric secret weapon from the Sequencers pack. Set melody lane to 16 steps, rhythm lane to 15, bass lane to 11 → each lane progresses at its own rate → infinite non-repeating patterns from simple inputs. Condition feature adds rule-based triggering (every 3rd pass, etc.).

key_parameters.name: Rate
key_parameters.description: Arpeggio speed — tempo-synced

key_parameters.name: Velocity (per step)
key_parameters.description: Velocity override for each step

Complex arpeggiated patterns with evolving wavetable timbres
Widens and thickens arpeggiated output
Echo on complex arp patterns creates intricate rhythmic webs
Spatializes complex arp sequences into immersive soundscapes

starter_recipes.name: Complex Arp
starter_recipes.description: 8-step arp with varying velocities and gates for dynamic movement

starter_recipes.name: Step Sequence Arp
starter_recipes.description: Hybrid sequencer-arpeggiator with per-step pitch offsets

starter_recipes.name: Probability Arp
starter_recipes.description: Random-direction arp with per-step probability for generative evolution

gotchas: New in Live 12 — not available in earlier versions
Per-step Pitch Offset stacks on top of the arpeggio's chord-tone cycling — the final pitch is chord note + offset
More CPU-intensive than the classic Arpeggiator due to per-step processing
Steps with Gate at 0% are silent — different from Probability at 0% which is a deterministic rest
Differs from Melodic Steps: Step Arp cycles through held chord tones, Melodic Steps applies a fixed pitch pattern
devices.sonic_description: Step-based arpeggiator combining the chord-cycling behavior of Arpeggiator with per-step control over pitch offset, velocity, gate, and probability. Offers more granular control than the classic Arpeggiator for complex, evolving arp patterns.

devices.name: Velocity

key_parameters.name: Drive
key_parameters.description: Amplifies the velocity curve — boosts or attenuates velocity values

key_parameters.name: Compand
key_parameters.description: Compresses (negative) or expands (positive) the velocity range

key_parameters.name: Random

key_parameters.name: Operation
key_parameters.description: How velocity is processed — Clip, Gate, or Fixed

pairs_well_with.reason: Normalizes velocity across arpeggiated notes for even dynamics
Controls hit dynamics for consistent drum patterns
MIDI velocity compression before audio compression for maximum control
Random pitch combined with random velocity for organic generative output

starter_recipes.name: Flatten Velocity
starter_recipes.description: Force all notes to the same velocity for mechanical, consistent output

starter_recipes.name: Expand Dynamics
starter_recipes.description: Exaggerate velocity differences for more expressive playing

starter_recipes.name: Random Humanize
starter_recipes.description: Add subtle random velocity variation to mechanical MIDI for human feel

starter_recipes.name: Soft Limiter
starter_recipes.description: Cap maximum velocity to prevent harsh hits while preserving dynamics

gotchas: Fixed operation mode ignores input velocity entirely — all notes output at (Out Hi + Out Low) / 2
Gate mode silences notes below Out Low — notes either pass or are killed, no middle ground
Random offset is bidirectional — it can push notes above Out Hi or below Out Low before clipping
Compand at extreme negative values squashes all velocities to a narrow band — check the curve display
Drive and Compand interact — set one at a time to understand the combined curve shape
devices.sonic_description: Processes and reshapes MIDI note velocity, offering compression, expansion, limiting, randomization, and fixed-value output. Controls dynamic expression and can flatten, exaggerate, or humanize the velocity range of incoming MIDI data.

devices.name: Harmonic Drone Generator

key_parameters.name: Tuning System
key_parameters.description: Selects the scale used to map voices. Each tuning produces a different harmonic flavor.

key_parameters.name: Voice Count
key_parameters.description: How many of the 8 voices are active. Fewer voices = purer drone; more voices = denser harmonic cloud.

key_parameters.name: Per-voice pitch
key_parameters.description: Each voice has its own pitch assignment. Spread across the scale or stack on root for subtle detuned bed.

key_parameters.name: Detune
key_parameters.description: Global detune amount. Low values (5-15 cents) give tape-warble; high values break into chord clouds.

key_parameters.name: Amp Envelope
key_parameters.description: Long attack (5-10s) for slow-swell pads; short attack for staccato 'one note' hits.

gotchas: All 8 voices at full level clip the output — use Voice Count or the internal level control
Long attack envelopes don't trigger instantly — for stabs, use short attack or pre-gate
Solfeggio frequencies are outside standard tuning — this is intentional; don't try to match to a song's key
devices.sonic_description: 8-voice Max for Live drone synth by Berlin-based Expert Math (shipped inside Drone Lab). Each voice has its own tuning assignment, and the device supports multiple tuning systems — Solfeggio frequencies, Pythagorean, Indonesian Pelog, Indian temperaments, plus equal and just intonation. Produces sustained harmonic drones with microtonal beating that equal- tempered synths architecturally cannot generate. The secret weapon for a harmonic bed that sits under a whole track at -30 dB and makes it sound like nothing else.

devices.name: Re-Enveloper

key_parameters.name: Band Split Frequencies
key_parameters.description: Low/Mid and Mid/High crossover points in Hz.

key_parameters.name: C/E Factor (per band)
key_parameters.description: Compression (positive) or Expansion (negative) factor per band.

key_parameters.name: Attack / Release (per band)
key_parameters.description: Envelope-shape time constants for each band independently.

gotchas: Expansion factors above 4.0 can cause clipping — compensate output gain.

devices.sonic_description: Multiband envelope processor with three adjustable frequency bands. Unlike compression (which reduces amplitude when over threshold), Re-Enveloper reshapes envelope character itself — attack, sustain, decay — independently per band. The right tool for 'shorten the decay of this sustained sound so it behaves like a stab' or 'expand the transient on the highs only'.

devices.name: Pitch Hack

key_parameters.description: Delay in ms or tempo-synced subdivisions.

key_parameters.description: Pitched echo feedback. Cascading when transpose is non-zero.

key_parameters.description: Reverse-plays the delay buffer.

devices.sonic_description: Versatile pitch-shifting delay for Creative Extensions. Combines transposition, reverse, feedback, and randomization on a single delay line. Lighter-weight companion to PitchLoop89 — less of a spatial architect, more of a per-sound treatment for pitched echoes and random reversal textures. The go-to for Akufen-style micro-stutter on hats and the random-reverse pitched ghosts that Villalobos uses on every track.

devices.sonic_description: Spectral halo/reverb hybrid. Creates reverb-like textures by blurring user-defined frequency ranges into dense clouds of delayed/smeared harmonic content. Not a real reverb — no IR — but the result behaves like one, with more control over which frequency ranges get haloed. Freeze button holds the current spectral content indefinitely. The tool for ethereal pads from sparse sources and alien textures that don't sound like rooms.

Used live for build-a-song-on-stage sets (Ed Sheeran, KT Tunstall style), in studio as a creative "sample yourself" tool, or for dub-style tape-loop emulation.

The go-to for Akufen-style micro-stutter on hats and the random-reverse pitched ghosts that Villalobos uses on every track.

The right tool for 'shorten the decay of this sustained sound so it behaves like a stab' or 'expand the transient on the highs only'.

devices.sonic_description: Spectral halo/reverb hybrid. Creates reverb-like textures by blurring user-defined frequency ranges into dense clouds of delayed/smeared harmonic content. Not a real reverb — no IR — but the result behaves like one, with more control over which frequency ranges get haloed. Freeze button holds the current spectral content indefinitely. The tool for ethereal pads from sparse sources and alien textures that don't sound like rooms.

devices.sonic_description: 8-voice Max for Live drone synth by Berlin-based Expert Math (shipped inside Drone Lab). Each voice has its own tuning assignment, and the device supports multiple tuning systems — Solfeggio frequencies, Pythagorean, Indonesian Pelog, Indian temperaments, plus equal and just intonation. Produces sustained harmonic drones with microtonal beating that equal- tempered synths architecturally cannot generate. The secret weapon for a harmonic bed that sits under a whole track at -30 dB and makes it sound like nothing else.
